questions.question: Explain the difference between an internal command and an external command in UNIX. Provide an example of each.
questions.hint: Internal commands are shell built-ins, while external commands are executed by the shell.

questions.question: Which of the following is an example of an internal command in UNIX? (a) ls command, (b) echo command, (c) cd command, (d) grep command
questions.hint: cd command is a shell built-in command.

questions.question: What is the effect of using the -c option with an external command in UNIX? (a) It allows the command to be executed in the background, (b) It allows the command to be executed in the foreground, (c) It allows the command to be executed without creating a new process, (d) It allows the command to be executed without creating a new shell.
questions.hint: The -c option allows the command to be executed without creating a new process.
